Subject: A missed-call intake pilot idea for {{company_name}}

Hi {{contact_name}},

I'm Chandru Vasudevan, founder of JVT Technologies LLC.

I came across {{company_name}} while looking at {{lead_context}}. One practical pilot I am putting in front of small offices right now is an AI receptionist intake layer for missed calls, after-hours requests, and repetitive callback details.

The first version is intentionally narrow:

- one phone path or callback workflow
- one intake packet format
- one email handoff to a human reviewer
- no legal, tax, medical, or financial advice from the assistant

The current pilot test is $750 setup plus $300-$500 per month for support and tuning. Before anything goes live, I can show a dry-run example using synthetic callers so the workflow and guardrails are clear.

Demo page: {{site_url}}/ai-receptionist-intake-demo.html

If that would be useful for {{company_name}}, I can send a one-page pilot scope instead of asking for a meeting first.

Best,
{{sender_name}}
{{sender_title}}
{{sender_company}}

# Workflow Cleanup Review-Only Pilot Template

Status: internal drafting only. Do not send from this file without explicit approval.

## Subject

`One workflow cleanup idea for [Company]`

## Draft

Hi [Name],

I am reaching out with a narrow workflow idea, not a broad AI pitch.

JVT helps small teams clean up one messy intake, inbox, meeting, or follow-up workflow at a time. The goal is to turn scattered notes, ad hoc drafts, and manual copy-paste steps into one reviewed packet and one visible queue.

The first version stays small:

- one workflow only
- one reviewed packet format
- one missing-information checklist
- one follow-up rule
- one human review checkpoint on anything client-facing

Typical starting point: `$1,500` fixed scope for one workflow cleanup.

If useful, the first thing to review would be a synthetic workflow-cleanup proof packet rather than a generic AI deck.

Best,

Chandru  
JVT Technologies LLC  
`hello@jvt-technologies.com`

## Positioning Notes

- Keep the message tied to one visible workflow pain.
- Do not imply autonomous customer communication.
- Do not promise deep integrations in the first pilot.
- Use only after recipient quality and fit have been manually reviewed.

Visual direction: dark JVT page, split screen between messy meeting notes and a clean action packet. Avoid robot imagery.

Script:

> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.
>
> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.
>
> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.
>
> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.
>
> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.
>

- due dates
- missing information
- draft follow-up

That is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.

## Content Goal

Show that JVT's "AI receptionist" offer is not a gimmick. The hook is:

> The assistant does not make promises. It captures the call, structures the
> request, and routes it to a human.

## YouTube Shorts Script

Length target: 35-45 seconds

Visual direction: dark JVT site background, quick cuts of synthetic packet
fields, no human/client footage required.

Script:

> Most small offices do not need an AI that pretends to be a person.
>
> They need missed calls turned into clean handoff packets.
>
> Name. Callback number. What happened. Urgency. Missing details. Who should
> review it next.
>
> That is what this JVT demo shows.
>
> A caller asks for help. The system captures the request, discloses the AI
> handoff, avoids legal or financial advice, and creates a packet a real person
> can act on.
>
> The point is not replacing staff.
>
> The point is no more mystery voicemails and no more lost context after hours.

On-screen text:

- "Missed call -> review packet"
- "No promises"
- "No advice"
- "Human handoff"
- "Built for document-heavy offices"

## Instagram Reel Caption

Small offices do not need AI that pretends to be a person.

They need missed calls turned into clean review packets:

- caller details
- request summary
- urgency
- missing info
- routing recommendation
- human handoff

That is the JVT AI receptionist wedge: capture the call, avoid risky answers,
and give staff something usable.

Review-first automation, not AI theater.

## Thumbnail / Cover Brief

Text:

> Missed calls should not become mystery voicemails.

Visual:

- dark JVT background
- phone/call card on left
- structured intake packet on right
- accent colors from current JVT palette
- avoid robot-face or generic AI iconography

## Content Goal

Show the concrete business outcome: calls and meetings should produce reviewed tasks, owner assignments, missing-info lists, and client-ready follow-up drafts.

Hook:

> Meetings are not done when the call ends. They are done when the next actions are captured.

## YouTube Shorts Script

Length target: 35-45 seconds

Visual direction: dark JVT page, split screen between messy meeting notes and a clean action packet. Avoid robot imagery.

Script:

> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.
>
> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.
>
> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.
>
> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.
>
> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.
>
> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.

On-screen text:

- "Meeting -> action packet"
- "Owners"
- "Due dates"
- "Missing info"
- "Human review before send"

## Instagram Reel Caption

A meeting is not finished just because the call ended.

The useful output is a reviewed packet:

- decisions
- task owners
- due dates
- missing information
- draft follow-up

That is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.

## Thumbnail / Cover Brief

Text:

> Stop losing action items after client calls.

Visual:

- dark JVT background
- messy notes/card stack on left
- clean action packet on right
- use current teal/blue/warm accent palette
- no generic AI face, brain, or robot icon

## Approval Checklist

- Use only synthetic meeting material.
- Do not claim autonomous client communication.
- Keep legal, tax, financial, insurance, and medical advice boundaries explicit when relevant.
- Mention human review before any follow-up is sent.
- Link to the public demo only after final review.
- Do not publish until operator approves exact copy and platform.

## Next Step

Record one short demo walkthrough and manually review it before any platform upload or API automation.

## Subject

`One workflow cleanup idea for [Company]`

## Draft

Hi [Name],

I am reaching out with a narrow workflow idea, not a broad AI pitch.

JVT helps small teams clean up one messy intake, inbox, meeting, or follow-up workflow at a time. The goal is to turn scattered notes, ad hoc drafts, and manual copy-paste steps into one reviewed packet and one visible queue.

The first version stays small:

- one workflow only
- one reviewed packet format
- one missing-information checklist
- one follow-up rule
- one human review checkpoint on anything client-facing

Typical starting point: `$1,500` fixed scope for one workflow cleanup.

If useful, the first thing to review would be a synthetic workflow-cleanup proof packet rather than a generic AI deck.

Best,

Chandru  
JVT Technologies LLC  
`hello@jvt-technologies.com`

## Positioning Notes

- Keep the message tied to one visible workflow pain.
- Do not imply autonomous customer communication.
- Do not promise deep integrations in the first pilot.
- Use only after recipient quality and fit have been manually reviewed.

# Synthetic CPA Onboarding Packet

Updated: 2026-05-20

## Scenario

Harbor Ledger CPA receives a new small-business client inquiry. The prospect needs bookkeeping cleanup, payroll review, and quarterly tax planning.

## Raw Intake Notes

- Company: Harbor Bike Repair LLC
- Contact: Morgan Lee
- Entity: Single-member LLC taxed as disregarded entity
- Books: QuickBooks Online, not reconciled since January
- Payroll: Gusto, two employees, one contractor
- Deadline: wants cleanup before June sales-tax filing
- Missing: bank statements, payroll reports, prior-year return, sales-tax login
- Risk: contractor classification may need CPA review

## Generated Work Packet

### Client Summary

Harbor Bike Repair LLC needs a bookkeeping cleanup and payroll review before the next sales-tax filing. The client appears to have incomplete bank reconciliations and missing payroll/source documents.

### Missing Documents

- January through current bank statements.
- QuickBooks Online accountant access.
- Gusto payroll reports.
- Prior-year tax return.
- Sales-tax portal access or exported filing history.
- Contractor agreement and payment summary.

### Internal Task List

- Admin: send document request email.
- Bookkeeping: review bank feed and reconciliation gap.
- Payroll reviewer: compare Gusto payroll totals to books.
- CPA: review contractor-classification issue before advice is given.
- Client owner: confirm filing deadline and urgency.

### Draft Client Email

Hi Morgan,

To scope the cleanup accurately, please send January through current bank statements, QuickBooks accountant access, Gusto payroll reports, the prior-year return, and sales-tax filing access or exports.

We will review the contractor-payment issue internally before making any recommendation.

Best,
Harbor Ledger CPA

## Review Boundary

# Synthetic Workflow Cleanup Review Packet

Updated: 2026-06-12

Status: synthetic internal proof. Review-only. Do not present as a live client deployment.

## Scenario

Summit Advisory Group has one painful admin workflow:

- a lead form comes in
- discovery-call notes live in email and notebooks
- document requests are typed from scratch
- follow-ups depend on memory
- AI drafts are used ad hoc, but nobody trusts them

The owner wants one fixed-scope cleanup before paying for a broader system.

## Current Workflow Snapshot

1. Website form sends an email to the office manager.
2. The office manager copies the contact into a spreadsheet.
3. Discovery-call notes are added later by hand.
4. Missing-document requests are drafted from scratch.
5. Follow-up timing depends on whoever remembers.
6. No-response leads disappear without a clean state or queue.

## Friction Found

- the same intake facts get retyped multiple times
- there is no single owner once the first email is answered
- missing documents are not tracked in one checklist
- AI drafts exist, but there is no review rule or standard template
- no-response follow-ups are inconsistent

## Proposed Future State

- one intake queue for new requests
- one reviewed summary packet after every form or call
- one missing-information checklist
- one follow-up draft template
- one visible status model: `new`, `needs-docs`, `scheduled`, `in-review`, `follow-up`, `closed`
- one weekly no-response queue for manual review

## Fixed-Scope JVT Pilot

Pilot shape: one workflow, one team, one review boundary

Target price: `$1,500` fixed scope

Target timeline: `10 business days`

### 10-Day Plan

- Days 1-2: map the current workflow and list repeated handoffs
- Days 3-4: define allowed statuses, owners, and review checkpoints
- Days 5-7: build the intake packet, missing-doc checklist, and follow-up draft
- Days 8-9: test the flow on synthetic or internal sample requests
- Day 10: tune rules, handoff notes, and operating checklist

## Example Reviewed Output

### Intake Summary

Prospect requested bookkeeping cleanup and monthly advisory support after a discovery call. The owner wants a proposal this week, but the team is missing bank statements, payroll access details, and the current bookkeeping close date.

### Missing-Information Checklist

- latest bank statements
- bookkeeping platform access details
- payroll provider and last payroll date
- current month close status
- preferred proposal deadline

### Internal Routing

- office manager: send the missing-doc checklist
- delivery lead: review scope fit and note likely cleanup size
- owner: confirm whether proposal or paid diagnostic is the next step
- follow-up queue: reopen in three business days if the documents have not arrived

### Draft Follow-Up For Review

Hi [Client Name],

Thanks again for the call. Before we scope the cleanup accurately, please send the latest bank statements, bookkeeping access details, payroll-provider information, and the current month close status. Once those arrive, we can confirm the right next step and timeline.

Best,
[Team]

## Success Scorecard

- every new request has an owner
- first response happens from one template instead of scratch
- missing documents are tracked in one checklist
- no-response items appear in a visible review queue
- ad hoc AI drafts become a reviewed workflow step instead of an invisible side habit

## Review Boundary

- no unreviewed client messaging
- no automated pricing promises
- no legal, tax, financial, or compliance advice
- staff remain responsible for external communication and scope decisions

## Sales Use

Use this as a review-only proof asset for the workflow cleanup lane. The promise is not "full automation." The promise is one messy workflow turned into a tighter operating system with review checkpoints.

# Synthetic Law-Firm Intake Packet

Updated: 2026-05-20

## Scenario

North Bridge Elder Law receives a call from an adult child asking about Medicaid planning and updating estate documents for a parent.

## Raw Intake Notes

- Caller: Dana Patel
- Relationship: adult child of potential client
- Potential client: parent, age 78
- Need: update will, understand Medicaid planning, review power of attorney
- Timeline: wants consult within two weeks
- Documents available: old will, bank statements, deed, pension letter
- Missing: current medication/care plan, long-term-care insurance details, existing POA
- Risk: caller asks what assets should be moved now

## Generated Work Packet

### Intake Summary

Dana Patel is seeking an elder-law consultation for a parent involving estate-document updates, Medicaid planning questions, and review of existing authority documents. The request is time-sensitive but not an emergency based on the intake notes.

### Documents To Request

- Existing will and codicils.
- Existing power of attorney and healthcare directive.
- Recent bank and investment statements.
- Deed or property ownership documents.
- Pension/social-security income letter.
- Long-term-care insurance documents.
- Current care-plan or facility information, if applicable.

### Internal Routing

- Intake coordinator: send document checklist and conflict-check form.
- Attorney/paralegal: review whether Medicaid-planning consult is appropriate.
- Scheduler: offer consultation windows within two weeks.
- Attorney only: respond to asset-transfer questions.

### Draft Client-Facing Reply

Hi Dana,

Thank you for reaching out. Before the consultation, please send any existing estate documents, power-of-attorney documents, recent financial statements, property documents, and long-term-care insurance information.

An attorney will need to review the asset-transfer questions directly, so we will not address those by intake email.

Best,
North Bridge Elder Law

## Review Boundary

This packet is intake support only. It does not provide legal advice, Medicaid planning advice, or asset-transfer recommendations.

# JVT Technologies Revenue Opportunities

Updated: 2026-05-13

This file is the recurring research ledger for opportunities outside the first private AI document-workflow offer. The rule is simple: pursue service lines that can be delivered by a small AI-assisted team, can reuse the existing M4 control-panel/agent infrastructure, and solve a concrete operational pain.

## Current Signals

- Businesses are using AI but still need help turning it into reliable operations. Zapier-linked reporting in early 2026 emphasized that teams spend real time cleaning up poor AI outputs, which supports a managed-AI-services angle instead of selling generic prompts.
- Voice and speech-native agents are now technically viable for production, but live calling adds provider setup, API cost, consent/compliance, and quality-control requirements.
- Twilio/OpenAI-style voice stacks can support inbound receptionist/intake workflows, but cost and reliability need to be modeled before offering fixed-price unlimited service.
- Reddit/small-business discussions point to setup friction as the real pain: businesses know automation is possible, but they do not want to wire together Gmail, Drive, CRM, forms, invoicing, and AI themselves.

Sources:
- OpenAI Realtime API: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-realtime/
- Twilio Voice pricing: https://www.twilio.com/en-us/voice/pricing/us
- Zapier RevOps automation report: https://zapier.com/resources/guides/state-of-revops-automation
- AI cleanup/workslop reporting: https://www.techradar.com/pro/cleaning-up-ai-workslop-is-costing-businesses-hundreds-of-hours-a-week

## Opportunity Shortlist

| Rank | Service line | Target customer | Pain | Offer | Pricing hypothesis | Delivery complexity | Risk | Next validation step |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Managed AI workflow cleanup | Accounting, law, property management, staffing | Staff try ChatGPT but outputs need review, formatting, and source checking | Build and maintain repeatable AI workflows with human review checkpoints | $750-$2,500 setup + $300-$1,500/mo support | Medium | Scope creep if every workflow is custom | Add a website section and one outreach variant focused on "AI cleanup and workflow reliability" |
| 2 | Speech-to-text / meeting-to-action packets | Small service firms, consultants, agencies | Calls and meetings create notes, tasks, follow-ups, and docs manually | Secure transcript intake, summary, action list, CRM/email draft, document packet | $250-$1,000/mo depending on volume | Low-medium | Transcription accuracy and privacy expectations | Demo with Chandru's DS677 audio assets and one client-style sample |
| 3 | AI receptionist / voice intake agent | Solo/small professional offices | Missed calls, repetitive intake questions, slow follow-up | Inbound voice agent that captures caller details, qualifies need, and creates review packet | $500-$2,000 setup + usage + $300-$1,000/mo | Medium-high | Live phone compliance, hallucination, escalation failure | Keep current voice agent dry-run; add public demo before selling live calling |
| 4 | Document packet generator | Law/accounting/insurance teams | Repetitive proposals, onboarding packets, checklists, and letters | Upload notes/templates -> generate consistent client-ready packets for review | $1,000-$3,500 setup + $500-$2,000/mo | Medium | Template edge cases and legal/tax review boundaries | Turn existing document demo into two vertical-specific demos |
| 5 | Inbox/document triage agent | Any document-heavy office | Shared inboxes receive attachments, requests, and noisy notifications | Monitor inbox, classify messages, extract docs, create task queue, draft responses | $1,500-$5,000 setup + $500-$2,500/mo | Medium | Email auth/access friction and privacy | Reuse JVT mailbox listener as internal proof |
| 6 | Managed local/private AI deployment | Privacy-sensitive small teams | They want AI but do not want client docs in random SaaS tools | Local/private retrieval assistant on Mac/mini-PC/server with admin console | $2,500-$10,000 setup + support retainer | High | Support burden and hardware variability | Package the M4 architecture as a case study |

## Immediate Recommendation

Do not chase crypto/trading/NFT-style passive income as a primary business line right now. The highest-confidence path is service revenue from workflow automation because JVT already has working assets: website, outreach engine, control panel, mailbox listener, document demo, and dry-run voice intake.

The next best add-on to the current offer is `Speech-to-text / meeting-to-action packets` because it is easy to demo, has low delivery complexity, and naturally feeds into document workflows.

## 2026-05-13 Reality Check

- Current outbound signal is weak: 108 sent packets, 0 tracked prospect replies, and 51 approved packets queued.
- This does not prove JVT is a bad idea, but it does prove generic private-document-assistant cold outbound should not be the main validation motion.
- The next target should be 5 real owner/operator conversations per week, not more automated sends.
- Prioritize sellable wedges with obvious pain: missed-call intake, meeting-to-action packets, inbox/document triage, and workflow automation cleanup.
- Keep `Private or local AI assistant` as a premium offer, but do not lead with it unless the prospect already has privacy-sensitive document pain.
- Trading/Alpaca experiment should remain paper-only until it has 60-90 days of logged paper results, drawdown tracking, and strict order controls.
- Creating a coin/NFT is not recommended as a revenue line. It requires community, liquidity, trust, distribution, and legal/compliance review; without those, it is closer to speculation than a service business.

| 2 | Meeting-to-action packets | Consultants, agencies, accounting/advisory teams | Sample audio -> transcript -> decisions -> tasks -> follow-up email | Operators who publish consultation/discovery-call offers | $300/mo or $75/meeting |
| 3 | Workflow automation cleanup | Small businesses using email/forms/sheets/CRM manually | Before/after admin workflow map | Owner/operators with visible lead forms and repetitive intake | $1,500 fixed project |
| 4 | Inbox/document triage | Law, accounting, insurance, property management | JVT mailbox listener case study | Shared-inbox offices with attachment-heavy workflows | $1,500 setup + $500/mo |
| 5 | Document packet generator | Law/accounting/insurance | Intake notes -> polished packet demo | Firms with onboarding/checklist/proposal workflows | $1,000 setup + $500/mo |

## 2026-05-06 Ops Note

- Outreach remains the primary validation path: 98 total sent packets, 14 approved packets waiting for explicit send authorization, and 0 imported prospect replies so far.
- Lead research quality is intentionally conservative. The latest model-screen pass rejected the available candidate, so growth needs better source discovery rather than weaker filtering.
- The next revenue add-on to validate remains speech-to-text / meeting-to-action packets because it can reuse the voice-intake and document-packet assets without live calling risk.

## Research Backlog

- Validate current transcription API pricing and compare OpenAI, Deepgram, AssemblyAI, Whisper local, and Apple Speech if relevant.
- Find 25 businesses that sell transcription/meeting-note services and compare positioning/pricing.
- Build one demo using Chandru-recorded audio: raw audio -> transcript -> summary -> follow-up email -> task checklist.
- Add one website section: "Turn calls and meetings into clean client-ready work packets."
- Create one outbound segment for consultants/agencies/accounting teams that mention meetings, onboarding, or client calls.
- Define red lines: no medical diagnosis, no legal/tax advice, no autonomous contract commitments, no outbound AI calls without opt-in/compliance review.

Do not post, schedule, upload, or connect platform APIs without approval.\n\nSource asset: `site/meeting-to-action-demo.html`\n\n## Content Goal\n\nShow the concrete business outcome: calls and meetings should produce reviewed tasks, owner assignments, missing-info lists, and client-ready follow-up drafts.\n\nHook:\n\n> Meetings are not done when the call ends. They are done when the next actions are captured.\n\n## YouTube Shorts Script\n\nLength target: 35-45 seconds\n\nVisual direction: dark JVT page, split screen between messy meeting notes and a clean action packet. Avoid robot imagery.\n\nScript:\n\n> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.\n>\n> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.\n>\n> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.\n>\n> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.\n>\n> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.\n>\n> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.\n\nOn-screen text:\n\n- \"Meeting -> action packet\"\n- \"Owners\"\n- \"Due dates\"\n- \"Missing info\"\n- \"Human review before send\"\n\n## Instagram Reel Caption\n\nA meeting is not finished just because the call ended.\n\nThe useful output is a reviewed packet:\n\n- decisions\n- task owners\n- due dates\n- missing information\n- draft follow-up\n\nThat is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.\n\n## Thumbnail / Cover Brief\n\nText:\n\n> Stop losing action items after client calls.\n\nVisual:\n\n- dark JVT background\n- messy notes/card stack on left\n- clean action packet on right\n- use current teal/blue/warm accent palette\n- no generic AI face, brain, or robot icon\n\n## Approval Checklist\n\n- Use only synthetic meeting material.\n- Do not claim autonomous client communication.\n- Keep legal, tax, financial, insurance, and medical advice boundaries explicit when relevant.\n- Mention human review before any follow-up is sent.\n- Link to the public demo only after final review.\n- Do not publish until operator approves exact copy and platform.\n\n## Next Step\n\nRecord one short demo walkthrough and manually review it before any platform upload or API automation.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Do not send from this file without explicit approval.\n\n## Subject\n\n`One workflow cleanup idea for [Company]`\n\n## Draft\n\nHi [Name],\n\nI am reaching out with a narrow workflow idea, not a broad AI pitch.\n\nJVT helps small teams clean up one messy intake, inbox, meeting, or follow-up workflow at a time. The goal is to turn scattered notes, ad hoc drafts, and manual copy-paste steps into one reviewed packet and one visible queue.\n\nThe first version stays small:\n\n- one workflow only\n- one reviewed packet format\n- one missing-information checklist\n- one follow-up rule\n- one human review checkpoint on anything client-facing\n\nTypical starting point: `$1,500` fixed scope for one workflow cleanup.\n\nIf useful, the first thing to review would be a synthetic workflow-cleanup proof packet rather than a generic AI deck.\n\nBest,\n\nChandru  \nJVT Technologies LLC  \n`hello@jvt-technologies.com`\n\n## Positioning Notes\n\n- Keep the message tied to one visible workflow pain.\n- Do not imply autonomous customer communication.\n- Do not promise deep integrations in the first pilot.\n- Use only after recipient quality and fit have been manually reviewed.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
ops/agent-control/epics/logs/20260613T052255Z-2026-06-12-review-only-pilot-packet-batch/codex-events.jsonl:38:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' strategy/content-ops/meeting-to-action-content-packet-2026-06-11.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Content Ops Packet: Meeting-To-Action Demo\n\nGenerated: 2026-06-11T19:43:49+00:00\n\nStatus: review-only. Do not post, schedule, upload, or connect platform APIs without approval.\n\nSource asset: `site/meeting-to-action-demo.html`\n\n## Content Goal\n\nShow the concrete business outcome: calls and meetings should produce reviewed tasks, owner assignments, missing-info lists, and client-ready follow-up drafts.\n\nHook:\n\n> Meetings are not done when the call ends. They are done when the next actions are captured.\n\n## YouTube Shorts Script\n\nLength target: 35-45 seconds\n\nVisual direction: dark JVT page, split screen between messy meeting notes and a clean action packet. Avoid robot imagery.\n\nScript:\n\n> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.\n>\n> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.\n>\n> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.\n>\n> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.\n>\n> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.\n>\n> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.\n\nOn-screen text:\n\n- \"Meeting -> action packet\"\n- \"Owners\"\n- \"Due dates\"\n- \"Missing info\"\n- \"Human review before send\"\n\n## Instagram Reel Caption\n\nA meeting is not finished just because the call ended.\n\nThe useful output is a reviewed packet:\n\n- decisions\n- task owners\n- due dates\n- missing information\n- draft follow-up\n\nThat is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.\n\n## Thumbnail / Cover Brief\n\nText:\n\n> Stop losing action items after client calls.\n\nVisual:\n\n- dark JVT background\n- messy notes/card stack on left\n- clean action packet on right\n- use current teal/blue/warm accent palette\n- no generic AI face, brain, or robot icon\n\n## Approval Checklist\n\n- Use only synthetic meeting material.\n- Do not claim autonomous client communication.\n- Keep legal, tax, financial, insurance, and medical advice boundaries explicit when relevant.\n- Mention human review before any follow-up is sent.\n- Link to the public demo only after final review.\n- Do not publish until operator approves exact copy and platform.\n\n## Next Step\n\nRecord one short demo walkthrough and manually review it before any platform upload or API automation.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:25:| 2 | Speech-to-text / meeting-to-action packets | Small service firms, consultants, agencies | Calls and meetings create notes, tasks, follow-ups, and docs manually | Secure transcript intake, summary, action list, CRM/email draft, document packet | $250-$1,000/mo depending on volume | Low-medium | Transcription accuracy and privacy expectations | Demo with Chandru's DS677 audio assets and one client-style sample |
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:26:| 3 | AI receptionist / voice intake agent | Solo/small professional offices | Missed calls, repetitive intake questions, slow follow-up | Inbound voice agent that captures caller details, qualifies need, and creates review packet | $500-$2,000 setup + usage + $300-$1,000/mo | Medium-high | Live phone compliance, hallucination, escalation failure | Keep current voice agent dry-run; add public demo before selling live calling |
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:27:| 4 | Document packet generator | Law/accounting/insurance teams | Repetitive proposals, onboarding packets, checklists, and letters | Upload notes/templates -> generate consistent client-ready packets for review | $1,000-$3,500 setup + $500-$2,000/mo | Medium | Template edge cases and legal/tax review boundaries | Turn existing document demo into two vertical-specific demos |
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:28:| 5 | Inbox/document triage agent | Any document-heavy office | Shared inboxes receive attachments, requests, and noisy notifications | Monitor inbox, classify messages, extract docs, create task queue, draft responses | $1,500-$5,000 setup + $500-$2,500/mo | Medium | Email auth/access friction and privacy | Reuse JVT mailbox listener as internal proof |
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:33:Do not chase crypto/trading/NFT-style passive income as a primary business line right now. The highest-confidence path is service revenue from workflow automation because JVT already has working assets: website, outreach engine, control panel, mailbox listener, document demo, and dry-run voice intake.
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:35:The next best add-on to the current offer is `Speech-to-text / meeting-to-action packets` because it is easy to demo, has low delivery complexity, and naturally feeds into document workflows.
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:39:- Current outbound signal is weak: 108 sent packets, 0 tracked prospect replies, and 51 approved packets queued.
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:42:- Prioritize sellable wedges with obvious pain: missed-call intake, meeting-to-action packets, inbox/document triage, and workflow automation cleanup.
strategy/revenue-opportunities.md:43:- Keep `Private or local AI assistant` as a premium offer, but do not lead with it unless the prospect already has privacy-sensitive document pain.

strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:21:- Target customer: solo and small professional offices that miss calls or repeat the same intake questions.
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:22:- Pain: missed calls and weak intake notes slow follow-up and leak opportunities.
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:23:- Offer: inbound AI intake that discloses itself, captures caller details, qualifies the request, and routes a review packet.

ops/agent-control/epics/logs/20260612T210659Z-2026-06-12-jvt-10k-revenue-engine/codex-events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' strategy/service-line-execution-board.md"","aggregated_output":"# JVT Service-Line Execution Board

Updated: 2026-05-27

Goal: create sellable proof assets and real conversation paths for JVT's near-term service wedges.

Success metric: 5 real owner/operator conversations per week and at least 1 paid-pilot proposal path.

## Active Wedges

| Priority | Wedge | First proof asset | Pricing test | Next action |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | AI receptionist / voice intake | Public dry-run intake demo page with three synthetic scenarios and paid-pilot homepage panel | $750 setup + $300-$500/mo support, usage billed separately | Send the paid-pilot outreach variant to verified missed-call-heavy prospects |
| 2 | Meeting-to-action packets | Public synthetic transcript-to-action packet demo page + proof PDF | $300/mo or $75 per meeting packet | Use the new prospect list to build a dedicated outreach wave |
| 3 | Workflow automation cleanup | Before/after map using JVT's own ops | $1,500 fixed project | Turn the workflow map into a one-page public case study |
| 4 | Inbox and document triage | JVT mailbox listener case study | $1,500 setup + $500/mo support | Turn the sanitized case study into a public sample |
| 5 | Document packet generator | Synthetic CPA and law-firm packets | $1,000 setup + $500/mo support | Package examples for website or PDF download |

## Completed Today

- Sent 10 quality-gated follow-up emails to older no-reply prospects.
- Fixed sender daily-limit accounting so follow-ups count by `sent_at`, not filename date.
- Raised sender defaults to 5 packets/run and 10 total outbound/day.
- Confirmed the new cold-wave generator currently has no eligible undeduped public-email leads.
- Added smart crypto mining as a speculative feasibility track, not an active JVT service wedge.
- AI receptionist / voice intake: created 3 synthetic caller scenarios and ran them through the dry-run `/api/test-intake` endpoint.
- Generated intake packets for missed-sales-call, existing-client-admin-request, and wrong-fit-caller scenarios.
- Published public demo pages for AI receptionist / voice intake and meeting-to-action packets.
- Added watchdog and follow-up status surfaces to the control panel.
- Added follow-up packet generation for older no-reply sent outreach.
- Created downloadable proof PDFs for AI receptionist and meeting-to-action demos.
- Built offer-specific candidate lists for AI receptionist and meeting-to-action.
- Wrote the inbox/document triage case study.
- Wrote the lead-to-follow-up workflow automation map.
- Created synthetic CPA onboarding and law-firm intake packet examples.
- Added a concrete AI receptionist paid-pilot panel to the homepage.
- Created the `ai-receptionist-paid-pilot.md` outreach variant.

## Red Lines

- No autonomous legal, tax, medical, financial, or investment advice.
- No outbound AI calls without opt-in/compliance review.
- No prospect emails without explicit authorization.
- No spending or provider signup without approval.
- No crypto mining hardware purchase, miner process, live trading, or custody setup without separate approval.

## Speculative Revenue Tracks

| Track | Status | Next validation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Smart crypto mining / profitability monitor | Research only | Build a read-only profitability calculator before considering any hardware or mining process |

def read_csv_rows(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    if not path.exists():
        return []
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as handle:
        return list(csv.DictReader(handle))


def priority_packet_review_queue(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    source = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / "priority-packet-candidates-2026-06-10.csv"
    rows = read_csv_rows(source)
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-packet-prep" / f"priority-review-queue-{today_slug()}.md"
    lines = [
        "# Priority Packet Review Queue",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
        "Status: internal queue staging only. This is not approval to send.",
        "",
        f"Source: `{source.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)}`",
        "",
        "## Queue Rules",
        "",
        "- Re-verify the public recipient before any packet becomes send-ready.",
        "- Reject placeholders, suspicious scraped addresses, unrelated personal addresses, mismatched domains, and generic page-title company names.",
        "- Confirm no recent reply, suppression, or same-thread conflict.",
        "- Keep each message tied to one concrete workflow pain.",
        "- Do not send from this file.",
        "",
    ]
    if not rows:
        lines.append("No priority rows were available.")
    for index, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
        lines.extend([
            f"## {index}. {row.get('company_name', 'Unknown')}",
            "",
            f"- Offer lane: {row.get('offer_lane', '')}",
            f"- Website: {row.get('website', '')}",
            f"- Candidate recipient: `{row.get('public_email', '')}`",
            f"- Current status: `{row.get('current_status', '')}`",
            f"- Why selected: {row.get('why_selected', '')}",
            f"- Required review before send: {row.get('required_review_before_send', '')}",
            f"- Next packet action: {row.get('next_packet_action', '')}",
            "",
            "Packet stance:",
            "",
            "- One narrow paid-pilot ask.",
            "- Human review remains explicit.",
            "- Avoid broad \"AI transformation\" language.",
            "- Include only the relevant proof asset/demo link after final review.",
            "",
        ])
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)], "candidate_count": len(rows)}


def offer_segment_summary(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    sources = [
        REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / "ai-receptionist-intake-targets.csv",
        REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / "meeting-to-action-targets.csv",
    ]
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / f"offer-segment-review-{today_slug()}.md"
    lines = [
        "# Offer Segment Review",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
        "Status: internal segmentation only. This is not a send list.",
        "",
    ]
    total = 0
    for source in sources:
        rows = read_csv_rows(source)
        total += len(rows)
        status_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
        for row in rows:
            key = row.get("lead_status") or row.get("verification_status") or "unknown"
            status_counts[key] = status_counts.get(key, 0) + 1
        lines.extend([
            f"## {source.name}",
            "",
            f"- Rows: `{len(rows)}`",
            f"- Source: `{source.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)}`",
            "",
            "Status mix:",
            "",
        ])
        if status_counts:
            for status, count in sorted(status_counts.items()):
                lines.append(f"- `{status}`: {count}")
        else:
            lines.append("- No rows available.")
        lines.extend(["", "Top review candidates:", ""])
        for row in rows[:5]:
            company = row.get("company_name", "Unknown")
            fit = row.get("offer_fit", "").strip()
            next_step = row.get("next_step", "").strip()
            lines.append(f"- {company}: {fit} Next: {next_step}")
        lines.append("")
    lines.extend([
        "## Next Validation Action",
        "",
        "Use this summary to decide which offer lane gets the next manually reviewed packet batch. Do not treat any row as send-ready until recipient quality is re-verified.",
        "",
    ])
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)], "row_count": total}


def ten_k_execution_digest(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "venture-outputs" / f"{today_slug()}-10k-execution-digest.md"
    venture = load_json(STATE_ROOT / "latest-venture-pipeline.json", {})
    eom = load_json(STATE_ROOT / "latest-eom-brief.json", {})
    runner = load_json(STATE_ROOT / "latest-local-task-runner.json", {})
    lines = [
        "# JVT $10k Execution Digest",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
        "Target: `$10,000 gross cash collected by 2027-03-31`",
        "",
        "## Current Operating Thesis",
        "",
        "The fastest path remains service-led: sell narrow paid pilots using proof assets, then attach monthly managed support. Trading, mining, tokens, hardware, and franchise ideas stay research-only until their risk-adjusted math beats one more paid service pilot.",
        "",
        "## What The Agents Should Push Next",
        "",
        "1. Convert demo/proof assets into review-only packet material.",
        "2. Re-verify recipients before anything enters a send-ready queue.",
        "3. Keep follow-ups active; no-reply is a queue state, not a conclusion.",
        "4. Refresh paper trading as research only.",
        "5. Generate one new venture scout or proof asset per day.",
        "",
        "## Latest Machine State",
        "",
        f"- Venture pipeline status: `{venture.get('status', 'unknown')}`",
        f"- Ranked opportunities: `{venture.get('summary', {}).get('opportunity_count', 'unknown')}`",

    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)]}


def venture_scout_index(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    source_dir = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "venture-scout"
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "venture-scout" / "INDEX.md"
    reports = sorted(source_dir.glob("*.md")) if source_dir.exists() else []
    lines = [
        "# Venture Scout Index",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
    ]
    if reports:
        for report in reports:
            title = report.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines()[0].lstrip("# ").strip()
            lines.append(f"- [{title}]({report.name})")
    else:
        lines.append("- No venture scout reports found.")
    lines.append("")
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)], "report_count": len(reports)}

Updated: 2026-06-12

## Objective

Create the clearest internal path to `$10,000` in gross cash collected by `2027-03-31` using productized JVT service work, not speculative revenue or approval-gated external actions.

## Operating Rules

- Stay service-led.
- Stop at internal proof, review packets, queue prep, and implementation planning.
- Do not send, post, buy, apply, fund, trade live, create wallets, mine, stake, or make outside commitments.

## Revenue Ladder

| Priority | Lane | First cash unit | Attach path | Current proof state | Main gap after this epic |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Workflow cleanup | `$1,500` fixed scope | support retainer after the first workflow works | Workflow map existed; this epic adds a sellable proof packet and demo page | Review-only packet batch aimed at real candidate companies |
| 2 | AI receptionist / intake | `$750` setup + `$300-$500/mo` | usage expansion and more call paths | Strong demo page, proof PDF, offer copy, and prospect lane already exist | Review-only packet batch and live setup prerequisites checklist |
| 3 | Meeting-to-action packets | `$75` per packet or `$300/mo` | recurring packet volume and add-on document work | Strong demo page, proof PDF, and prospect lane already exist | Download-ready sample packet and second-angle packet batch |
| 4 | Inbox and document triage | `$1,500` setup + `$500/mo` | more inboxes, draft replies, attachment routing | Internal case study exists | Synthetic before/after sample and public-safe proof surface |
| 5 | Document packet generator | `$1,000` setup + `$500/mo` | template expansion and monthly packet work | Two synthetic packet examples already exist | Packaging and vertical framing comparable to stronger lanes |

## Base-Case Mix To Clear $10k

This mix is illustrative. It is meant to keep the target concrete and service-led.

| Unit mix | Cash contribution |
| --- | --- |
| 2 workflow cleanup projects at `$1,500` | `$3,000` |
| 2 AI receptionist pilots at `$750` setup + `$350` first month | `$2,200` |
| 2 meeting-to-action pilots at `$450` blended first unit | `$900` |
| 1 inbox triage pilot at `$2,000` blended setup + month one | `$2,000` |
| 1 document packet generator setup at `$1,500` | `$1,500` |
| 1 add-on support month or extra packet batch | `$500` |
| Total | `$10,100` |

## Current Priority Order

### 1. Workflow cleanup

Why now:

- lowest delivery dependency
- easiest fixed-scope price point to explain
- strongest path to cash without live phone or mailbox access

### 2. AI receptionist / intake

Why next:

- best existing proof stack
- clear paid-pilot shape
- needs internal packetization, not more abstract planning

### 3. Meeting-to-action packets

Why next:

- cheapest pilot
- easiest attach offer after a workflow cleanup or intake conversation
- reuses transcript and packet assets already built

### 4. Inbox and document triage

Why later in the sequence:

- attractive recurring support
- still behind the top lanes in public-safe proof packaging

### 5. Document packet generator

Why as an attach lane:

- useful once trust exists
- weaker as the first wedge than workflow cleanup or intake

## Execution Map

### Stage 1: Prove

- Keep one review-safe proof artifact per lane.
- Make the workflow obvious.
- Keep boundary language explicit.

### Stage 2: Package

- One narrow paid-pilot shape per lane.
- One proof link or packet.
- One pricing band.
- One review boundary.

### Stage 3: Queue

- Use the local task runner for repeat refreshes and summaries.
- Use the epic agent for multi-file packet batches, proof-gap closure, and lane packaging.

### Stage 4: Manual close path

- Manual review decides which lane gets outbound attention.
- Only after explicit approval should any packet move toward real send or public use.

This epic stops at Stage 3.

## Queue Split

### Queue next in local task runner

- `offer_segment_summary`
- `priority_packet_review_queue`
- `ten_k_execution_digest`

These are recurring refresh tasks. They are safe, internal, and already supported.

### Queue next in epic agent

- review-only paid-pilot packet batch for the strongest candidate lanes
- inbox triage proof packaging
- document packet generator packaging

These are larger multi-artifact stories that benefit from bounded Codex workspace-write execution.

## Success Checkpoints

- 3 lanes have proof plus pricing plus review boundary plus queue path.
- 1 next epic is already queued for review-only packet work.
- local task runner has refresh work staged for the new execution path.
- architect only needs to choose which lane gets manual review priority first.

## Repository References

- Plan anchor: `strategy/jvt-10k-by-mar-2027-plan.md`
- Child-story specs: `strategy/jvt-revenue-engine-child-stories-2026-06-12.md`
- Workflow cleanup proof packet: `client-work/synthetic-examples/workflow-cleanup-review-packet.md`
- Workflow cleanup demo surface: `site/workflow-cleanup-demo.html`
- Next queued epic: `ops/agent-control/epics/queued/2026-06-12-review-only-pilot-packet-batch.json`

Offer lane: AI receptionist / intake

> Missed closing-status calls into clean intake packets

Title and settlement offices receive time-sensitive buyer, seller, agent, and
document-status questions. JVT can position this as capture-and-route only, not
advice or autonomous decision-making.

Proof asset to include after review:

- AI receptionist / intake demo

- Avoid claims about handling legal or escrow decisions.

## 2. Matus Law Group

Offer lane: AI receptionist / intake

Draft subject angle:

> Intake summaries for estate-planning calls

Why this fits:

Estate and elder-law firms receive urgent intake calls and family follow-ups.
The safe wedge is caller capture, structured summary, and human review.

Proof asset to include after review:

- AI receptionist / intake demo
- Guardrail note: no legal advice, no autonomous commitments

- Clean location metadata.
- Keep legal-advice boundary explicit.

## 3. Stone & Company LLC

Offer lane: meeting-to-action packets

Draft subject angle:

> Client calls into clean task and document-request packets

Why this fits:

CPA client meetings create decisions, missing-document requests, deadline tasks,
and follow-up emails. The meeting-to-action offer is clearer than a generic AI

Proof asset to include after review:

- Meeting-to-action demo

Review notes before queue staging:

- Avoid tax-advice automation claims.

## 4. Atteign

Offer lane: meeting-to-action packets

Draft subject angle:

> Advisory calls into reviewed follow-up packets

Why this fits:

Advisory/accounting calls create follow-up tasks, missing-document asks, and
client recap emails. JVT can position this as reviewed back-office support.

Proof asset to include after review:

- Meeting-to-action demo
- Example packet language focused on owner/operator review

Review notes before queue staging:

- Confirm no recent reply or suppression condition.
- Verify `info@atteign.com` remains public.
- Keep scope to notes/tasks/follow-up drafts.

## 5. Sound Management

Offer lane: AI receptionist / intake

Draft subject angle:

> Maintenance and rental calls into routed intake summaries

Why this fits:

Property management teams receive maintenance, rental, callback, and tenant
questions that can be captured and routed without promising answers.

Proof asset to include after review:

- AI receptionist / intake demo

Hi {{contact_name}},

I'm Chandru Vasudevan, founder of JVT Technologies LLC.

I came across {{company_name}} while looking at {{lead_context}}. One practical pilot I am putting in front of small offices right now is an AI receptionist intake layer for missed calls, after-hours requests, and repetitive callback details.

- one phone path or callback workflow
- one intake packet format
- one email handoff to a human reviewer
- no legal, tax, medical, or financial advice from the assistant

Demo page: {{site_url}}/ai-receptionist-intake-demo.html

If that would be useful for {{company_name}}, I can send a one-page pilot scope instead of asking for a meeting first.

Best,
{{sender_name}}

Do not post, schedule, upload, or connect platform APIs without approval.\n\nSource asset: `site/meeting-to-action-demo.html`\n\n## Content Goal\n\nShow the concrete business outcome: calls and meetings should produce reviewed tasks, owner assignments, missing-info lists, and client-ready follow-up drafts.\n\nHook:\n\n> Meetings are not done when the call ends. They are done when the next actions are captured.\n\n## YouTube Shorts Script\n\nLength target: 35-45 seconds\n\nVisual direction: dark JVT page, split screen between messy meeting notes and a clean action packet. Avoid robot imagery.\n\nScript:\n\n> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.\n>\n> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.\n>\n> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.\n>\n> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.\n>\n> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.\n>\n> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.\n\nOn-screen text:\n\n- \"Meeting -> action packet\"\n- \"Owners\"\n- \"Due dates\"\n- \"Missing info\"\n- \"Human review before send\"\n\n## Instagram Reel Caption\n\nA meeting is not finished just because the call ended.\n\nThe useful output is a reviewed packet:\n\n- decisions\n- task owners\n- due dates\n- missing information\n- draft follow-up\n\nThat is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.\n\n## Thumbnail / Cover Brief\n\nText:\n\n> Stop losing action items after client calls.\n\nVisual:\n\n- dark JVT background\n- messy notes/card stack on left\n- clean action packet on right\n- use current teal/blue/warm accent palette\n- no generic AI face, brain, or robot icon\n\n## Approval Checklist\n\n- Use only synthetic meeting material.\n- Do not claim autonomous client communication.\n- Keep legal, tax, financial, insurance, and medical advice boundaries explicit when relevant.\n- Mention human review before any follow-up is sent.\n- Link to the public demo only after final review.\n- Do not publish until operator approves exact copy and platform.\n\n## Next Step\n\nRecord one short demo walkthrough and manually review it before any platform upload or API automation.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Avoid robot imagery.\\n\\nScript:\\n\\n> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.\\n>\\n> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.\\n>\\n> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.\\n>\\n> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.\\n>\\n> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.\\n>\\n> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.\\n\\nOn-screen text:\\n\\n- \\\"Meeting -> action packet\\\"\\n- \\\"Owners\\\"\\n- \\\"Due dates\\\"\\n- \\\"Missing info\\\"\\n- \\\"Human review before send\\\"\\n\\n## Instagram Reel Caption\\n\\nA meeting is not finished just because the call ended.\\n\\nThe useful output is a reviewed packet:\\n\\n- decisions\\n- task owners\\n- due dates\\n- missing information\\n- draft follow-up\\n\\nThat is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.\\n\\n## Thumbnail / Cover Brief\\n\\nText:\\n\\n> Stop losing action items after client calls.\\n\\nVisual:\\n\\n- dark JVT background\\n- messy notes/card stack on left\\n- clean action packet on right\\n- use current teal/blue/warm accent palette\\n- no generic AI face, brain, or robot icon\\n\\n## Approval Checklist\\n\\n- Use only synthetic meeting material.\\n- Do not claim autonomous client communication.\\n- Keep legal, tax, financial, insurance, and medical advice boundaries explicit when relevant.\\n- Mention human review before any follow-up is sent.\\n- Link to the public demo only after final review.\\n- Do not publish until operator approves exact copy and platform.\\n\\n## Next Step\\n\\nRecord one short demo walkthrough and manually review it before any platform upload or API automation.\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\nops/agent-control/epics/done/2026-06-12-jvt-10k-revenue-engine.json:22:    \"Create at least one review-only proof or packet artifact if it can be done safely from existing material.\",\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:7:## 1. Meeting-to-action packets
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:11:- Offer: audio or meeting recording to transcript, summary, follow-up draft, task checklist, and client-ready packet.
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:19:## 2. AI receptionist and intake
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:21:- Target customer: solo and small professional offices that miss calls or repeat the same intake questions.
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:22:- Pain: missed calls and weak intake notes slow follow-up and leak opportunities.
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:23:- Offer: inbound AI intake that discloses itself, captures caller details, qualifies the request, and routes a review packet.
strategy/jvt-service-line-todos.md:35:- Offer: convert ad hoc AI usage into repeatable workflows with inputs, review checkpoints, source checks, and handoff notes.

ops/agent-control/epics/logs/20260612T210659Z-2026-06-12-jvt-10k-revenue-engine/codex-events.jsonl:22:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_11","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' runbooks/jvt-epic-agent-operating-model.md\"","aggregated_output":"# JVT Epic Agent Operating Model\n\nThe epic agent is for large story-style work that should continue on the M4 Mac\nmini without asking the solution architect to do every low-level step.\n\n## Core Idea\n\nSmall recurring tasks stay in `ops/agent-control/tasks`.\n\nLarge durable stories go in `ops/agent-control/epics`.\n\nThe epic agent wakes on the M4, takes one queued epic at a time, runs bounded\nCodex CLI execution, writes logs, and creates an architect handoff. If the Mac\nis offline, the queue just waits on disk.\n\n## Directories\n\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/queued`\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/running`\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/done`\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/blocked`\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/held`\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/logs`\n- `ops/agent-control/epics/architect-inbox`\n\n## Execution Modes\n\n- `architect_brief`: write an architect handoff only.\n- `codex_readonly_plan`: run Codex CLI in read-only mode and capture a plan.\n- `codex_workspace_write`: run Codex CLI with repo workspace-write access.\n\nNo mode may spend money, send prospect email, post publicly, submit\napplications, modify financial accounts, trade live, move funds, create wallets,\nmine, stake, or make external commitments.\n\n## LaunchAgent\n\nInstall:\n\n```bash\nops/agent-control/install_epic_agent_runner_launch_agent.sh\n```\n\nManual run:\n\n```bash\npython3 ops/agent-control/epic_agent_runner.py --max-epics 1\n```\n\nStatus:\n\n- `ops/agent-control/state/latest-epic-agent.json`\n- `ops/agent-control/state/latest-epic-agent.md`\n- `ops/agent-control/state/epic-agent.out.log`\n- `ops/agent-control/state/epic-agent.err.log`\n\n## Architect Callback\n\nThe agent cannot directly speak into every live desktop thread. Instead it\nwrites callback material into:\n\n`ops/agent-control/epics/architect-inbox`\n\nThe solution architect reads that inbox, answers blockers, and queues follow-up\nepics when needed.\n\n## Epic Spec Minimum Fields\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"2026-06-12-example\",\n  \"title\": \"Example Epic\",\n  \"execution_mode\": \"codex_workspace_write\",\n  \"codex_enabled\": true,\n  \"requires_approval\": false,\n  \"story\": \"Large story brief...\",\n  \"scope_paths\": [\"strategy/\", \"runbooks/\"],\n  \"deliverables\": [\"What the agent should create or update\"],\n  \"acceptance_criteria\": [\"How the agent knows the story is done\"],\n  \"timeout_seconds\": 1800\n}\n```\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

def read_csv_rows(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    if not path.exists():
        return []
    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", newline="") as handle:
        return list(csv.DictReader(handle))


def priority_packet_review_queue(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    source = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / "priority-packet-candidates-2026-06-10.csv"
    rows = read_csv_rows(source)
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-packet-prep" / f"priority-review-queue-{today_slug()}.md"
    lines = [
        "# Priority Packet Review Queue",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
        "Status: internal queue staging only. This is not approval to send.",
        "",
        f"Source: `{source.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)}`",
        "",
        "## Queue Rules",
        "",
        "- Re-verify the public recipient before any packet becomes send-ready.",
        "- Reject placeholders, suspicious scraped addresses, unrelated personal addresses, mismatched domains, and generic page-title company names.",
        "- Confirm no recent reply, suppression, or same-thread conflict.",
        "- Keep each message tied to one concrete workflow pain.",
        "- Do not send from this file.",
        "",
    ]
    if not rows:
        lines.append("No priority rows were available.")
    for index, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
        lines.extend([
            f"## {index}. {row.get('company_name', 'Unknown')}",
            "",
            f"- Offer lane: {row.get('offer_lane', '')}",
            f"- Website: {row.get('website', '')}",
            f"- Candidate recipient: `{row.get('public_email', '')}`",
            f"- Current status: `{row.get('current_status', '')}`",
            f"- Why selected: {row.get('why_selected', '')}",
            f"- Required review before send: {row.get('required_review_before_send', '')}",
            f"- Next packet action: {row.get('next_packet_action', '')}",
            "",
            "Packet stance:",
            "",
            "- One narrow paid-pilot ask.",
            "- Human review remains explicit.",
            "- Avoid broad \"AI transformation\" language.",
            "- Include only the relevant proof asset/demo link after final review.",
            "",
        ])
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)], "candidate_count": len(rows)}


def offer_segment_summary(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    sources = [
        REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / "ai-receptionist-intake-targets.csv",
        REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / "meeting-to-action-targets.csv",
    ]
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "prospect-lists" / f"offer-segment-review-{today_slug()}.md"
    lines = [
        "# Offer Segment Review",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
        "Status: internal segmentation only. This is not a send list.",
        "",
    ]
    total = 0
    for source in sources:
        rows = read_csv_rows(source)
        total += len(rows)
        status_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
        for row in rows:
            key = row.get("lead_status") or row.get("verification_status") or "unknown"
            status_counts[key] = status_counts.get(key, 0) + 1
        lines.extend([
            f"## {source.name}",
            "",
            f"- Rows: `{len(rows)}`",
            f"- Source: `{source.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)}`",
            "",
            "Status mix:",
            "",
        ])
        if status_counts:
            for status, count in sorted(status_counts.items()):
                lines.append(f"- `{status}`: {count}")
        else:
            lines.append("- No rows available.")
        lines.extend(["", "Top review candidates:", ""])
        for row in rows[:5]:
            company = row.get("company_name", "Unknown")
            fit = row.get("offer_fit", "").strip()
            next_step = row.get("next_step", "").strip()
            lines.append(f"- {company}: {fit} Next: {next_step}")
        lines.append("")
    lines.extend([
        "## Next Validation Action",
        "",
        "Use this summary to decide which offer lane gets the next manually reviewed packet batch. Do not treat any row as send-ready until recipient quality is re-verified.",
        "",
    ])
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)], "row_count": total}

def venture_scout_index(_task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    source_dir = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "venture-scout"
    path = REPO_ROOT / "strategy" / "venture-scout" / "INDEX.md"
    reports = sorted(source_dir.glob("*.md")) if source_dir.exists() else []
    lines = [
        "# Venture Scout Index",
        "",
        f"Generated: {utc_now()}",
        "",
    ]
    if reports:
        for report in reports:
            title = report.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").splitlines()[0].lstrip("# ").strip()
            lines.append(f"- [{title}]({report.name})")
    else:
        lines.append("- No venture scout reports found.")
    lines.append("")
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
    return {"ok": True, "artifacts": [str(path)], "report_count": len(reports)}

Updated: 2026-06-12

## Objective

Create the clearest internal path to `$10,000` in gross cash collected by `2027-03-31` using productized JVT service work, not speculative revenue or approval-gated external actions.

## Operating Rules

- Stay service-led.
- Stop at internal proof, review packets, queue prep, and implementation planning.
- Do not send, post, buy, apply, fund, trade live, create wallets, mine, stake, or make outside commitments.

## Revenue Ladder

| Priority | Lane | First cash unit | Attach path | Current proof state | Main gap after this epic |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Workflow cleanup | `$1,500` fixed scope | support retainer after the first workflow works | Workflow map existed; this epic adds a sellable proof packet and demo page | Review-only packet batch aimed at real candidate companies |
| 2 | AI receptionist / intake | `$750` setup + `$300-$500/mo` | usage expansion and more call paths | Strong demo page, proof PDF, offer copy, and prospect lane already exist | Review-only packet batch and live setup prerequisites checklist |
| 3 | Meeting-to-action packets | `$75` per packet or `$300/mo` | recurring packet volume and add-on document work | Strong demo page, proof PDF, and prospect lane already exist | Download-ready sample packet and second-angle packet batch |
| 4 | Inbox and document triage | `$1,500` setup + `$500/mo` | more inboxes, draft replies, attachment routing | Internal case study exists | Synthetic before/after sample and public-safe proof surface |
| 5 | Document packet generator | `$1,000` setup + `$500/mo` | template expansion and monthly packet work | Two synthetic packet examples already exist | Packaging and vertical framing comparable to stronger lanes |

## Base-Case Mix To Clear $10k

This mix is illustrative. It is meant to keep the target concrete and service-led.

| Unit mix | Cash contribution |
| --- | --- |
| 2 workflow cleanup projects at `$1,500` | `$3,000` |
| 2 AI receptionist pilots at `$750` setup + `$350` first month | `$2,200` |
| 2 meeting-to-action pilots at `$450` blended first unit | `$900` |
| 1 inbox triage pilot at `$2,000` blended setup + month one | `$2,000` |
| 1 document packet generator setup at `$1,500` | `$1,500` |
| 1 add-on support month or extra packet batch | `$500` |
| Total | `$10,100` |

## Current Priority Order

### 1. Workflow cleanup

Why now:

- lowest delivery dependency
- easiest fixed-scope price point to explain
- strongest path to cash without live phone or mailbox access

### 2. AI receptionist / intake

Why next:

- best existing proof stack
- clear paid-pilot shape
- needs internal packetization, not more abstract planning

### 3. Meeting-to-action packets

Why next:

- cheapest pilot
- easiest attach offer after a workflow cleanup or intake conversation
- reuses transcript and packet assets already built

### 4. Inbox and document triage

Why later in the sequence:

- attractive recurring support
- still behind the top lanes in public-safe proof packaging

### 5. Document packet generator

Why as an attach lane:

- useful once trust exists
- weaker as the first wedge than workflow cleanup or intake

## Execution Map

### Stage 1: Prove

- Keep one review-safe proof artifact per lane.
- Make the workflow obvious.
- Keep boundary language explicit.

### Stage 2: Package

- One narrow paid-pilot shape per lane.
- One proof link or packet.
- One pricing band.
- One review boundary.

### Stage 3: Queue

- Use the local task runner for repeat refreshes and summaries.
- Use the epic agent for multi-file packet batches, proof-gap closure, and lane packaging.

### Stage 4: Manual close path

- Manual review decides which lane gets outbound attention.
- Only after explicit approval should any packet move toward real send or public use.

This epic stops at Stage 3.

## Queue Split

### Queue next in local task runner

- `offer_segment_summary`
- `priority_packet_review_queue`
- `ten_k_execution_digest`

These are recurring refresh tasks. They are safe, internal, and already supported.

### Queue next in epic agent

- review-only paid-pilot packet batch for the strongest candidate lanes
- inbox triage proof packaging
- document packet generator packaging

These are larger multi-artifact stories that benefit from bounded Codex workspace-write execution.

## Success Checkpoints

- 3 lanes have proof plus pricing plus review boundary plus queue path.
- 1 next epic is already queued for review-only packet work.
- local task runner has refresh work staged for the new execution path.
- architect only needs to choose which lane gets manual review priority first.

- Plan anchor: `strategy/jvt-10k-by-mar-2027-plan.md`
- Child-story specs: `strategy/jvt-revenue-engine-child-stories-2026-06-12.md`

- Workflow cleanup demo surface: `site/workflow-cleanup-demo.html`
- Next queued epic: `ops/agent-control/epics/queued/2026-06-12-review-only-pilot-packet-batch.json`

ops/agent-control/epics/logs/20260613T052255Z-2026-06-12-review-only-pilot-packet-batch/codex-events.jsonl:38:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' strategy/content-ops/meeting-to-action-content-packet-2026-06-11.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Content Ops Packet: Meeting-To-Action Demo\n\nGenerated: 2026-06-11T19:43:49+00:00\n\nStatus: review-only. Do not post, schedule, upload, or connect platform APIs without approval.\n\nSource asset: `site/meeting-to-action-demo.html`\n\n## Content Goal\n\nShow the concrete business outcome: calls and meetings should produce reviewed tasks, owner assignments, missing-info lists, and client-ready follow-up drafts.\n\nHook:\n\n> Meetings are not done when the call ends. They are done when the next actions are captured.\n\n## YouTube Shorts Script\n\nLength target: 35-45 seconds\n\nVisual direction: dark JVT page, split screen between messy meeting notes and a clean action packet. Avoid robot imagery.\n\nScript:\n\n> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.\n>\n> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.\n>\n> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.\n>\n> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.\n>\n> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.\n>\n> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.\n\nOn-screen text:\n\n- \"Meeting -> action packet\"\n- \"Owners\"\n- \"Due dates\"\n- \"Missing info\"\n- \"Human review before send\"\n\n## Instagram Reel Caption\n\nA meeting is not finished just because the call ended.\n\nThe useful output is a reviewed packet:\n\n- decisions\n- task owners\n- due dates\n- missing information\n- draft follow-up\n\nThat is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.\n\n## Thumbnail / Cover Brief\n\nText:\n\n> Stop losing action items after client calls.\n\nVisual:\n\n- dark JVT background\n- messy notes/card stack on left\n- clean action packet on right\n- use current teal/blue/warm accent palette\n- no generic AI face, brain, or robot icon\n\n## Approval Checklist\n\n- Use only synthetic meeting material.\n- Do not claim autonomous client communication.\n- Keep legal, tax, financial, insurance, and medical advice boundaries explicit when relevant.\n- Mention human review before any follow-up is sent.\n- Link to the public demo only after final review.\n- Do not publish until operator approves exact copy and platform.\n\n## Next Step\n\nRecord one short demo walkthrough and manually review it before any platform upload or API automation.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Avoid robot imagery.\\n\\nScript:\\n\\n> Most teams do not lose work because nobody talked about it.\\n>\\n> They lose work because the call ends and the next steps scatter across memory, notes, email, and chat.\\n>\\n> This JVT demo turns a meeting into a reviewed action packet.\\n>\\n> Decisions. Owners. Due dates. Missing information. Draft follow-up.\\n>\\n> A real person still reviews it before anything goes out.\\n>\\n> The value is simple: fewer forgotten tasks, cleaner client follow-up, and less admin drag after every meeting.\\n\\nOn-screen text:\\n\\n- \\\"Meeting -> action packet\\\"\\n- \\\"Owners\\\"\\n- \\\"Due dates\\\"\\n- \\\"Missing info\\\"\\n- \\\"Human review before send\\\"\\n\\n## Instagram Reel Caption\\n\\nA meeting is not finished just because the call ended.\\n\\nThe useful output is a reviewed packet:\\n\\n- decisions\\n- task owners\\n- due dates\\n- missing information\\n- draft follow-up\\n\\nThat is the JVT meeting-to-action wedge: turn client calls into structured next steps without pretending the system should make business decisions on its own.\\n\\n## Thumbnail / Cover The draft set is in place. I’m reading the new files back now and checking the touched-path status so I can catch any formatting or scope issues before closing the epic.

`One post-call packet idea for [Company]`

## Draft

Hi [Name],

I am reaching out with one narrow workflow idea, not a broad AI pitch.

JVT helps small teams turn calls and meetings into reviewed action packets instead of scattered notes, memory, and ad hoc follow-up drafts. The useful output is a clean summary, clear owners, missing-information list, and a reviewed follow-up draft before anything client-facing goes out.

The first version stays small:

- one recurring meeting type
- one packet format
- one reviewed follow-up draft
- one owner and status model
- one human review checkpoint before any client message is sent

Typical starting point: `$75` per packet or `$300` per month for a small recurring batch.

If useful, the first thing to review would be a synthetic meeting-to-action proof packet rather than a generic AI deck.

Best,

Chandru  
JVT Technologies LLC  
`hello@jvt-technologies.com`

## Positioning Notes

- Keep the message tied to one specific post-call pain.
- Do not imply autonomous client communication.
- Do not promise deep integration or CRM writeback in the first pilot.
